untranslatable_reason: Time Series Momentum (TSMOM) requires: (1) cross-asset class momentum calculation across commodities, equities, bonds, and currencies simultaneously; (2) equal ex-ante volatility normalization/scaling applied dynamically across heterogeneous asset classes; (3) momentum reversal detection during specific crisis periods. The allowed indicators list contains single-asset price/technical indicators only and lacks multi-asset momentum compositing, cross-asset volatility normalization, and crisis regime detection. The signal cannot be accurately translated to single-contract conditions without losing the core cross-asset equal-volatility weighting methodology.
translation_notes: This is a sophisticated multi-asset class factor strategy that requires portfolio-level calculations (equal ex-ante vol weighting across 4+ asset classes) rather than individual contract logic. A proper implementation would need: (1) a momentum calculation function across asset universes, (2) dynamic volatility scaling per asset class, (3) regime-aware exit logic for crisis reversals. The simplified entry_long/entry_short using returns > 0 / < 0 captures direction but completely misses the equal vol-weighting and cross-asset portfolio construction that defines the strategy.
